One or two cells were placed in a closed cylindrical
metal holder which served as a Faraday-cup for the
initial beam current measurement. In front and at the
back of each cell 12.05 or 25 mm thick Ti foils were
attached to monitor the beam. At the end of the
bombardment the irradiated gas cells were first
allowed to cool for a couple of minutes. Afterwards
the closed cells are placed in an ice-water bath at
0 C for 5 min to allow settling of the produced Cs and
I isotopes onto the wall of the chamber before
starting the measurements. At the end of the

For estimation of the uncertainty of the
particle mean energy in the gas cells and in the
monitor foils the cumulative errors influencing the
homogeneity and thickness variation of the foils and
target gas, beam straggling) were taken into account.
Energy uncertainty varies between 0.2 and 0.7 MeV
depending on the number and thickness of the layers.
